aliases: Roti Jala (roti net, lace pancake, puteri mandi — alternate regional name)

malay: Roti Jala (roti net, lace pancake, puteri mandi — alternate regional name)

food_subgroup: Thin lace-patterned wheat flour pancake with coconut milk, egg, and turmeric

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for roti jala. Closest parent concept: pancake (FOODON:03315571).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code assigned for roti jala as a distinct entry. Closest parent: Cereal and cereal products (A0010000).
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for roti jala. Not a clinical substance requiring SNOMED mapping.

preparation_method: batter poured through 5-hole funnel mould onto hot griddle
lace/net pattern formed by circular pouring motion

serving.description: 4 pieces roti jala (~160g, roti jala only; not including curry dipping sauce)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of roti jala is 4 pieces (approximately 160g), typically folded into quarters or rolled and served alongside chicken curry (kari ayam) or beef rendang for dipping.

nutrient_source.title: Roti Jala — Institute for Medical Research Malaysia MyFCD (roti jala entry), supplemented by Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo for coconut milk fat profile
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR) — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD); Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Roti jala entry retrieved directly from IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Coconut milk saturated fat profile cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database. Both are approved primary regional sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from analogous thin wheat flour pancake with egg and coconut milk; reference: University of Sydney GI Database — pancake/crepe entries; egg and coconut milk fat content lowers GI relative to plain wheat crepe
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for roti jala exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 58 is estimated from the closest analogous food: thin wheat flour pancake/crepe (GI ~66 plain), adjusted downward to ~58 based on the fat and protein contribution of egg and coconut milk, which slow gastric emptying and reduce glycemic response. Thin lace structure increases surface area but coconut milk MCT moderates digestion rate. GL per 160g serving = (58 × 36.8g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 21.3. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.guidance: Roti jala contains approximately 180mg sodium per 100g (~288mg per 4-piece serving of 160g), making it one of the lowest-sodium Malaysian staple foods; individuals with hypertension should be mindful that the accompanying chicken curry dipping sauce contributes the majority of sodium in this meal and should be consumed in moderation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic load (~21 per 4-piece serving) from refined wheat flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roti jala has a medium GI (~58) and a glycemic load of approximately 21 per 4-piece serving (160g); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 2 pieces and pair with protein-rich curry to further blunt the glycemic response, as the refined wheat flour base lacks wholegrain fibre benefits.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (~3.5g per 100g; ~5.6g per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roti jala provides approximately 5.6g saturated fat per 4-piece serving, primarily from coconut milk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume in moderation as part of a balanced diet, noting that the trans fat content is negligible (0.0g) and the cholesterol contribution (~99mg per serving) is within acceptable limits for most CVD dietary guidelines when consumed occasionally.

rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Roti jala contains culinary-dose turmeric (~3–8mg curcumin per serving), which provides minimal but additive anti-inflammatory benefit within a Malaysian dietary pattern; the low sodium content is beneficial for managing systemic inflammation associated with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roti jala (literally 'net bread' in Malay) is a traditional Malaysian lace pancake made by pouring a thin yellow batter through a special 5-hole funnel mould in a circular motion over a hot griddle, creating an intricate lace or net pattern. The batter is composed of wheat flour, egg, coconut milk, turmeric (which gives the characteristic yellow colour), salt, and water. The pancake sets rapidly on the griddle, producing a thin, delicate, slightly crispy-edged crepe that is typically folded into quarters or rolled. Roti jala is a festive Malay food strongly associated with Eid celebrations (Hari Raya Aidilfitri and Hari Raya Aidiladha) and is also found at Malay hawker stalls and kopitiams in Singapore and Malaysia. It is traditionally served with rich chicken curry (kari ayam) or beef rendang for dipping — the lace structure traps curry sauce beautifully. Nutritionally, a 4-piece serving (160g) provides approximately 264 kcal, 8.8g protein, 9.3g fat, and 36.8g carbohydrate. Sodium is low at ~288mg per serving, making it one of the most sodium-friendly Malaysian staple foods. GI is approximately 58 (medium), with a glycemic load of ~21 per serving. Saturated fat (~5.6g per serving) comes primarily from coconut milk. The turmeric provides sub-therapeutic curcumin (~3–8mg per serving). Gluten-containing; suitable for vegetarians and halal diets. Source: IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: Nutritional values sourced directly from IMR Malaysia MyFCD (roti jala entry), an approved primary regional source under Project Veritas v2 source policy, supplemented by Singapore HPB for coconut milk fat profile. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous pancake entries with documented adjustment rationale. Confidence 1.0 on macros; 0.9 on GI estimate.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for roti jala not separately reported in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the MyFCD roti jala entry. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per MyFCD source — no hydrogenated fats used in traditional roti jala preparation). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed negligible — no vitamin C-containing ingredients in standard roti jala batter). All core macros (protein_g 5.5, fat_g 5.8, carbohydrate_g 23.0) are non-zero and well-supported. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database pancake/crepe entries, adjusted for egg and coconut milk fat content; confidence 0.9. Roti jala is a Malaysian Malay specialty; multilingual names provided for Singaporean diaspora context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Chinese name 罗惹网饼 / 羅惹網餅 is a descriptive transliteration used in Singapore Chinese-language contexts.
